title: ficus cassidyana var. casiguranensis
intro: Ficus cassidyana var. casiguranensis, also known as the Casiguran ficus, is a rare and endemic species of fig tree found in the Philippines. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful in treating various health conditions such as wounds, fever, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Ficus cassidyana var. casiguranensis include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting the immune system, making it a sought-after herb in traditional medicine. In herbal preparations, the leaves and roots of the plant are often used to make teas, infusions, and tonics, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
